And I'm anticipating objections because I'm selling at a distance, right? And one of them is this, right? We got a closet full of training programs. We've run consultants through here. We've done all of that, right? And we know, truth is, we won't implement. Now it's actually easier to raise it at a distance that it is one-to-one. This happens to be one that if you put it in the 12 reasons not to buy, the individual never really has to enunciate it to spot it. And well, I'm home alone. Nobody's going to know. I'll read the answer to this, right? So you can be pretty blunt and frank and candid about this because you're not confronting face-to-face. But I think it's not there. Of course it's there. They know. I was doing a consultation years ago. I was at Amway Corporation in Grand Rapids in a boardroom with 30, 35 executives. And the meeting was all about them doing infomercial style DVDs for the more expensive stuff they sell to leave. So Joe Distributor, who can't sell, won't sell, etc., just leaves the DVD with you. It sells the fire alarm. It sells the cookware. And one of the executives said an extremely perceptive thing. So one spoke up and said, well, we really only need the strategic help with this because we have a video studio that rivals Hollywood. We produce video all the time. And the other executive spoke up and said, if we do it in a house, we'll amwayize it and we'll wind up with exactly the same thing we have now, right? We will fuck it up. We need to get it out of here in the hands of entirely different people, right? And so he was willing to admit we are going to implement that, right? And so certainly with consumers, with weight loss, with health, with fitness, there's a lot of that, right? And you're exactly right. They have to be let off the hook, right? The blame has to go on the previous stuff, not on them. And there has to be an answer to why this is different. Absolutely. Well, the last one in this section, boy, we're running up against the clock on this one. But here's a classic. You know what it is. You know what we love it. I have to think about it. I've got to think about it. Nick? Sure. What do you need to think about? Is. The smart aleck in me. But I would say, you know, it goes back to the sales presentation. So there isn't an impetus to buy now. And maybe it's the bonuses that go away from the people or premiums that go away to make them act now. You want to move them towards really what their objective is, is it the benefit that they're moving forward or they're moving away from. And you know, some people need to think. They need to ponder. They just need to come up with it on their own mind. I don't want to leave it to those people. I want to make sure that we do a conditional sale. Let's move forward with this. And then if you think about it, just at the end of the day, isn't for you. We're going to move away from it. But it goes back to they don't trust themselves. And they're replaying past movies in their mind that weren't successful. And you need to move yourself through that. There's a town. Think it over Iowa. It has only a yellow traffic light. It's where all these people go right after they've told you to think it over as they go back home to think it over Iowa. So look, there are some purchases matched with some people that this is a legitimate thing. If you sell in rural Midwest and rural South, you will encounter evangelical Christians who have an oath that they do not make any sort of decision without going home and praying over it. They're telling the truth. And an acceptable response is usually not let's all get down on our knees and play together. There are people who pull that off, but not many. Who would it be one to pull that off? There are people who have an oath amongst themselves to sleep on it. So this is real. And you're not going to dissuade them. There are complicated purchases. More often than not, though, you've either bungled the lead up to it. So.
